website_ux_conversion.purpose: Improve user experience and increase the likelihood that visitors take the desired action on the page.
core_principles: Make websites simple, intuitive, and easy to navigate
Reduce clutter and guide users toward one clear action per page
Use a strong headline, short intro, and visible CTA above the fold
Structure pages clearly with hero, benefits, proof, and CTA sections
Use clear visual hierarchy with readable fonts, whitespace, and strong contrast
Optimize for mobile responsiveness and thumb-friendly navigation
Keep load time under 3 seconds when possible
Use testimonials, trust badges, reviews, and proof to support conversions
Keep forms short and easy to complete
Run A/B tests on headlines, CTA text, and button design
Use user behavior data like CTR, funnels, and heatmaps to improve pages
diagnostic_patterns: High traffic with low engagement usually indicates messaging mismatch, poor UX, or weak page clarity
High engagement with low conversion usually indicates friction in the conversion path
Low time on page usually indicates weak content relevance, poor structure, or low readability
Users viewing pages without taking action usually indicates weak CTA placement, weak value communication, or friction
recommended_levers: Improve above-the-fold messaging and page clarity
Strengthen CTA visibility and wording
Reduce friction in forms and conversion steps
Improve trust-building proof and visual hierarchy
Test layout, headline, CTA, and page flow changes

seo_keyword_strategy.purpose: Improve search visibility and click performance by aligning pages to keyword intent and search demand.
core_principles: Match each page to one primary keyword or topic and clear search intent
Use target keywords in title tag, meta description, URL, H1, early body copy, and image alt text
Keep meta titles around 50 to 60 characters
Keep meta descriptions under about 160 characters
Avoid keyword stuffing and prioritize natural language
Use internal links to connect related pages
Use descriptive, short URL slugs
Optimize images with descriptive file names and alt text
Use canonical tags to manage duplicate content
Use 301 redirects for removed or outdated pages
Improve crawlability, indexability, page speed, mobile usability, HTTPS, and site structure
Use local SEO tactics like Google Business Profile, reviews, and location-based keywords
Build backlinks and authority through quality referring domains, PR, partnerships, and useful content
diagnostic_patterns: High impressions with low CTR usually indicates weak title, meta description, or intent alignment
Low impressions usually indicates indexing, coverage, relevance, or demand issues
Strong rankings with low CTR usually indicates a SERP messaging opportunity
Weak rankings usually indicate content depth, relevance, competition, or authority issues
recommended_levers: Rewrite title tags and meta descriptions
Improve keyword-to-page intent alignment
Expand topical depth and supporting sections
Strengthen internal linking
Improve crawlability, page speed, and authority signals

aeo_geo_ai_search.purpose: Improve visibility in answer engines, AI-generated summaries, and AI-driven recommendations by making content easier to extract, trust, and cite.
core_principles: Focus on question-based keywords and real user questions
Structure headings as questions when relevant
Answer questions directly in the first 1 to 2 sentences under a heading
Keep direct answers concise and easy to quote
Use bullet points, numbered lists, and modular sections
Make each section understandable on its own
Use consistent formatting like definition to detail to example
Optimize content for featured snippets, People Also Ask, and AI Overviews
Use schema markup such as FAQ, HowTo, Article, and Local Business where relevant
Make content easy for AI systems to parse, extract, and cite
Prioritize clarity, structure, and trust over creativity in formatting
Add trust signals like expert insights, credentials, citations, statistics, and case studies
Build topical authority with clusters of related content
Keep important pages crawlable and accessible
Update content regularly so it stays fresh and relevant
Track AI visibility, citations, and brand mentions in addition to rankings and clicks
diagnostic_patterns: Pages that rank in search but are not easily quotable often need stronger AEO structure
Long content without clear answer blocks often performs poorly in answer extraction
Weak brand mentions or weak citation visibility usually indicate weak GEO authority
Content that is difficult to scan or parse usually performs worse in AI-driven search experiences
recommended_levers: Add FAQ sections and direct answer blocks
Use clearer headings and modular content structure
Improve trust signals with citations, stats, and expertise
Strengthen brand/entity mentions across the web
Refresh important pages for clarity, freshness, and extractability

social_media_content_marketing.purpose: Improve social media performance by aligning content to business goals, audience needs, and high-value engagement signals.
core_principles: Align social media goals with broader business objectives
Use audience personas to guide messaging and content choices
Post consistently and prioritize content quality and relevance
Create content that informs, entertains, inspires, or connects
Prioritize video and strong visuals for engagement
Focus on the first few seconds of content to stop scrolling
Optimize captions, bios, hashtags, highlights, and thumbnails with keywords
Track watch time, retention, engagement, profile clicks, and conversions
Test different content formats and repeat what performs best
Research trends, competitors, and niche content opportunities
Use stories, reels, live content, and platform-specific formats strategically
Encourage interaction through comments, polls, questions, and user-generated content
Treat social as both a visibility tool and a relationship-building channel
Use influencers, creators, UGC, paid social, retargeting, and lookalikes strategically
Link social content back to site content where appropriate
diagnostic_patterns: High reach with low engagement usually indicates weak content resonance or weak hook
High reach with low saves usually indicates low-value or low-depth content
High profile visits with low follows usually indicates weak positioning, weak trust, or weak profile conversion
High engagement with low growth usually indicates missing follow strategy or weak conversion path
recommended_levers: Improve hooks and first-frame relevance
Create more educational, relatable, or save-worthy content
Replicate top-performing topics and formats
Strengthen follow, click, and conversion CTAs
Improve profile positioning, bio clarity, and trust signals

analytics_digital_strategy.purpose: Use measurement and experimentation to improve marketing performance across channels and tie activity back to business outcomes.
core_principles: Align marketing activity with business goals and measurable KPIs
Use data to guide content, UX, SEO, and campaign decisions
Track acquisition, engagement, conversion, and retention metrics
Identify drop-off points and underperforming pages or channels
Use data for experimentation, iteration, and personalization
Treat digital strategy as a full business system, not just a channel tactic
Adapt quickly to trends, platform shifts, and customer behavior
Focus on customer value, not just tools or technology
Use experimentation and testing as part of ongoing optimization
Build scalable strategies through systems, partnerships, and continuous improvement
diagnostic_patterns: Traffic growth without engagement growth usually indicates a traffic quality or landing-page relevance issue
Engagement growth without conversion growth usually indicates UX or offer friction
Large tool discrepancies should trigger a measurement and attribution check before strategic conclusions
Channel underperformance should be analyzed in the context of funnel stage, audience quality, and business goals
recommended_levers: Use segmented analysis before making channel-level decisions
Prioritize high-impact experiments tied to business KPIs
Investigate drop-off points before recommending major strategy changes
Cross-check pre-click and post-click tools before diagnosing performance issues
Use data to prioritize opportunities by impact, effort, and business value
